rationale_for_baseline_tier: Benign tinea pedis, but the deep macerated fissure is a portal for cellulitis — prompt antifungal treatment and drying with a strong cellulitis/lymphangitis safety-net; not an emergency now.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Top of the foot/ankle becoming red, hot and swollen, red streaks up the leg, or fever/shivering
escalation_edges.rationale: Ascending cellulitis/lymphangitis through the fissure can progress to sepsis.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the top of your foot becomes red, hot and swollen, red streaks travel up your leg, or you get a fever or shivering — go to the emergency department promptly.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Shift worker in heavy occlusive boots
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Emphasise sock changes/drying/boot treatment to prevent recurrence.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is athlete's foot with a deep split. Use the antifungal cream on the whole foot daily for weeks and a brief combination cream on the crack, and keep the feet very dry (change socks mid-shift, air the feet, powder the boots). Watch for redness climbing up the foot/leg or a fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP in 7 days; ED promptly for ascending cellulitis/fever
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No urgent action needed unless ascending redness/red streaks/fever — then ED.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document interdigital/moccasin tinea pedis with a fissure (cellulitis screened negative), whole-foot antifungal + brief combination, drying/footwear advice, and a cellulitis safety-net.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: No cellulitis safety-net for an open macerated fissure.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A cellulitis/lymphangitis ascends from the fissure unnoticed and progresses to sepsis.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No antifungal/drying advice, so the fissure persists as an infection portal.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance for uncomplicated tinea pedis without ascending infection.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot KOH-scrape or palpate for early ascending cellulitis remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as tinea pedis with whole-foot antifungal + drying, and give a strong cellulitis safety-net given the open fissure.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Missing the cellulitis portal, or failing to keep the feet dry.
